title: A1 Review: Grammar Consolidation
description: A1-level grammar consolidation review. Practice nominative and accusative cases, present tense translations, vocabulary matching (food, family), common error correction (gender agreement, wrong case), a shopping dialogue, and a listening dictation. Linked to grammar rule GR-290.

exercises.prompt: Identify the nominative subject in the sentence.

data.question_cz: Které slovo je podmět v nominativu?
data.question_en: Which word is the nominative subject in: 'Babička vaří oběd.'?
data.explanation: 'Babička' is the subject in the nominative case. 'Babička vaří oběd.' = Grandma is cooking lunch. The subject performs the action and is in the nominative. 'Oběd' is the accusative object.

exercises.prompt: Choose the correct nominative form of the noun.

data.question_en: Choose the correct nominative form: '___ je hezký.' (a dog — pes, masculine animate)
data.explanation: 'Pes je hezký.' = The dog is nice. 'Pes' is the nominative singular of masculine animate 'pes'. The nominative is the base form used for subjects and after 'to je' (this is).

exercises.prompt: Identify the nominative form used after 'to je'.

data.question_cz: Který tvar se používá po 'to je'?
data.question_en: Choose the correct form: 'To je moje ___.' (a book — kniha, feminine)
data.explanation: 'To je moje kniha.' = This is my book. After 'to je' (this is), the noun is in the nominative case. 'Kniha' (feminine) stays in nominative 'kniha'. The adjective 'moje' also agrees in nominative feminine.
'Vidím ženu.' = I see a woman. Feminine nouns ending in -a change to -u in the accusative singular: žena → ženu.
'Mám nové auto.' = I have a new car. Neuter nouns like 'auto' have the same form in nominative and accusative singular: auto → auto. The adjective 'nové' also stays the same (neuter nom/acc).

'Hledám kamaráda.' = I'm looking for a friend. Masculine animate nouns add -a in the accusative singular: kamarád → kamaráda. This applies to all masculine animate nouns (muž → muže, bratr → bratra, pes → psa).

data.grammar_note: 'Piju kávu každé ráno.' = I drink coffee every morning. 'Piju' (or formal 'piji') = 1st person singular of 'pít' (to drink). 'Kávu' = coffee (accusative, feminine -a → -u). 'Každé ráno' = every morning (neuter accusative).
'Ona pracuje v bance.' = She works in a bank. 'Pracuje' = 3rd person singular of 'pracovat'. 'V bance' = in a bank (locative case after 'v'). 'Banka' (feminine) → locative 'bance' (-a → -e).

data.source: We are students and we study Czech.
accepted_answers: My jsme studenti a učíme se česky.
Jsme studenti a učíme se česky.
data.grammar_note: 'My jsme studenti a učíme se česky.' = We are students and we study Czech. 'Jsme' = are (1st pl. of být). 'Studenti' = students (nominative plural, masculine animate). 'Učíme se' = we study/learn (1st pl. + reflexive 'se'). 'Česky' = Czech (adverb).

exercises.answer_key: My jsme studenti a učíme se česky.

exercises.prompt: Match the Czech food words to their English meanings.

data.prompt_en: Match each Czech food word to its English meaning.
data.explanation: A1 food vocabulary: chléb (bread, masc.), mléko (milk, neut.), maso (meat, neut.), ovoce (fruit, neut.), zelenina (vegetable, fem.).

exercises.prompt: Match the Czech family words to their English meanings.

data.prompt_en: Match each Czech family word to its English meaning.
data.explanation: A1 family vocabulary: matka (mother, fem.), otec (father, masc. animate), bratr (brother, masc. animate), sestra (sister, fem.), babička (grandmother, fem.).

exercises.prompt: Find and correct the gender agreement error.

data.prompt_en: Find and correct the gender agreement error.
data.explanation: 'Kniha' is feminine, so the adjective must take the feminine ending '-á': 'hezká kniha' (nice book). 'To je hezká kniha.' = This is a nice book. Masculine: hezký (dům), feminine: hezká (kniha), neuter: hezké (auto).

exercises.prompt: Find and correct the case error.

data.prompt_en: Find and correct the case error.
data.explanation: The verb 'vidět' (to see) takes a direct object in the accusative case. 'Žena' (feminine) → accusative 'ženu'. 'Vidím ženu.' = I see a woman. Feminine nouns ending in -a change to -u in the accusative singular.

exercises.prompt: Complete the shopping dialogue. What does the customer answer to the shopkeeper?

lines.speaker: person_b
lines.text: Ano, ještě mléko. Kolik to je?

lines.speaker: person_b
lines.text: Tady jsou peníze. Děkuji, na shledanou!

data.explanation: Shopping dialogue at A1 level. Key phrases: 'Dobrý den, přejete si?' (Hello, what would you like?), 'Prosím si...' (I'd like...), 'Ještě něco?' (Anything else?), 'Kolik to je?' (How much is it?), 'Dohromady to je...' (Altogether it's...), 'Tady jsou peníze' (Here's the money).
blank_answers: Dobrý den, prosím si kilo jablek a dva rohlíky.
Prosím si kilo jablek a dva rohlíky.
Dobrý den, prosím si kilo jablek.

exercises.answer_key: Dobrý den, prosím si kilo jablek a dva rohlíky.

exercises.prompt: Listen and write what you hear.

data.expected_text: Bydlím v Praze a pracuji v bance.
data.note: A1 review sentence: 'Bydlím v Praze a pracuji v bance.' = I live in Prague and I work in a bank. Note 'v Praze' (locative after 'v') and 'v bance' (locative after 'v'). Both feminine nouns: Praha → Praze, banka → bance.

exercises.answer_key: Bydlím v Praze a pracuji v bance.
